title: Epicharis loheri
intro: Epicharis loheri, also known as Epicharis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potent antimicrobial, antitumor, and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Epicharis loheri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Epicharis loheri is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as coughs, colds, and skin infections.
